passage: Whoever ______ the most volunteer hours by the end of the fiscal year will receive a recognition award at the annual gala.
question: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
explanation: 'Whoever' is the subject of the dependent clause 'Whoever ______ the most volunteer hours by the end of the fiscal year,' which functions as the subject of the main verb 'will receive.' 'Whoever' is a singular indefinite pronoun, requiring a singular present-tense verb: 'logs.' 'Log' is first-person or plural. 'Logged' is past tense, which is inconsistent with the future-oriented context. 'Logging' is a gerund or present participle, not a finite verb.
